label: NAMS-Certified Menopause Practitioner
when_to_refer: First-line referral for moderate-to-severe vasomotor symptoms, mood or sleep changes, genitourinary symptoms, or any symptom cluster suggesting hormone therapy candidacy. The baseline specialist for peri, meno, and post-menopausal care.

label: Endocrinologist
when_to_refer: Indicated for early or premature ovarian insufficiency (POI), suspected thyroid dysfunction (cold extremities + hair thinning + fatigue cluster), significant insulin resistance, adrenal or thyroid co-pathology, or metabolic syndrome emerging alongside menopause symptoms.

label: Pelvic Floor Physical Therapist
when_to_refer: Stress urinary incontinence, urgency or frequency changes, pelvic organ prolapse symptoms, pain with intercourse attributable to pelvic floor tension, or any GSM symptom that has not resolved with over-the-counter vaginal moisturisers.

label: Urogynecologist
when_to_refer: Severe pelvic floor dysfunction, recurrent UTIs in the setting of GSM, pain with intercourse refractory to conservative measures, or suspected pelvic organ prolapse requiring surgical evaluation.

label: Cardiologist
when_to_refer: URGENT: palpitations at rest or during exertion, unexplained dyspnea, chest tightness, or exercise intolerance — particularly when cardiac symptoms co-occur with flash events. Cardiovascular risk rises sharply in the two years post-final menstrual period.

label: Mental Health Professional (Therapist / Psychiatrist)
when_to_refer: URGENT: score 3–4 on PHQ-9 equivalent items, acute panic attacks, clinical depression, suicidal ideation, or a feeling of profound identity disconnection. Hormone-driven mood disorders require coordinated psychiatric and menopause specialist co-management.

label: Sleep Medicine Specialist (CBT-I Provider)
when_to_refer: Severe sleep fragmentation unresponsive to sleep hygiene (>3 nocturnal wakings every night, total sleep <5 h), suspected sleep apnoea (snoring + witnessed apnoeas + morning headaches), or where CBT-I delivery is indicated before pharmacological intervention.

label: Sports Medicine Physician / Physiatrist
when_to_refer: Joint pain, myalgia, or movement limitations that impair resistance training compliance; pre-DEXA evaluation of fracture risk; management of tendinopathies that worsen with estrogen decline (e.g. frozen shoulder, plantar fasciitis).

label: Dermatologist
when_to_refer: Diffuse hair thinning or shedding that may indicate thyroid-adjacent alopecia, severe nodulocystic acne in perimenopause (androgen-shift driven), or skin conditions (rosacea, lichen sclerosus) requiring prescription-level management.

label: Registered Dietitian / Nutritionist
when_to_refer: Metabolic syndrome, significant weight gain around menopause transition, gut symptoms (bloating, irregular bowels, histamine reactions), or where the gut estrobolome and dietary fibre strategy need personalised clinical-grade planning.
